summary: DHH notes that Rails, 37signals, and himself are featured in both Wired and BusinessWeek on the same day. While flattered, he finds the experience surreal and reflects on the contrast between print media's heavy production process and the simplicity of blogging. He marvels at how much effort goes into short print pieces, including multiple photo sessions lasting hours.
key_points: Rails and 37signals received simultaneous coverage in two major publications, marking a milestone in mainstream recognition
Print media's wide distribution gives coverage a sense of gravitas that feels 'puffy' compared to online content
The production overhead of print magazines—hours of photo sessions for short articles—contrasts sharply with the immediacy of blogging
money_quotes: There's just something about paper and wide distribution that makes it all seem so puffy.
Imagine that they have to do all that work for so many small pieces.
Certainly a different world from just jotting your notes down on a blog and possibly slapping on a half-assed photo.
key_insight: The heavy production process of print media feels absurdly disproportionate compared to the effortless immediacy of blogging.
